rxn_class_summary: Cobalt-catalyzed electrooxidative dehydrogenative C-H/N-H [4+2] annulation of aromatic/vinyl amides with ethylene or ethyne to give dihydroisoquinolinones/isoquinolinones
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of substrate-coordinated Co(II) complex to Co(III) (and later Co(I) to Co(II)) regenerates the active high-valent cobalt catalyst, replacing a chemical oxidant

key_intermediates.name: Co(II)-amide complex (bidentate N,N-coordinated, 8-aminoquinoline directing group) - complex A
Co(III)-amide complex B formed by anodic oxidation of A
Cyclic Co(III) metallacycle C after C-H activation (CMD with pivalate)
Co(III) alkyl intermediate D after ethylene insertion
Co(I) species after reductive elimination, reoxidized at anode to Co(II)

electron_flow: Co(II) binds the bidentate amide with pivalate assistance; the anode oxidizes this Co(II) complex to Co(III), which performs intramolecular C-H activation (CMD with pivalate); ethylene/ethyne insertion followed by C-N reductive elimination releases product and Co(I), which is re-oxidized at the anode to Co(II); protons are reduced at the cathode to H2.
rate_determining_step: Electrochemical oxidation of the coordinated Co(II) complex to Co(III) (C-H cleavage is kinetically relevant but not rate-determining; only secondary KIE in parallel experiments)

probe_reagents.name: [D5]-1a (pentadeuterio benzamide) intermolecular competition
probe_reagents.observation: 3aa:[D4]-3aa = 3.5:1 in intermolecular competition, indicating C-H cleavage is kinetically relevant

probe_reagents.name: [D5]-1a parallel reactions
probe_reagents.observation: kH/kD = 1.4 (small, secondary); C-H cleavage is a slow step but not rate-determining

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a, Co(acac)2, and mixture
probe_reagents.observation: 1a oxidation at 1.47 V; Co(acac)2 at 1.12 and 1.53 V; Co(acac)2+1a shows stronger peak at 1.13 V indicating Co(II) is oxidized first; potential-controlled electrolysis at 1.13 V gave 3aa in 70% yield

probe_reagents.name: Kinetic order studies (variation of [1a], [NaOPiv], [Co], current)
probe_reagents.observation: Rate independent of [1a], [NaOPiv], and [Co]; rate depends on current, indicating electrochemical oxidation is rate-limiting

probe_reagents.name: GC headspace analysis
probe_reagents.observation: Large amount of H2 detected, confirming cathodic proton reduction

notes: Authors invoke prior EXAFS evidence (ref 31) that anodic oxidation of Co(acac)2 generates Co(III). Pivalate (HOPiv/NaOPiv) acts as CMD base for C-H activation. 8-Aminoquinoline (Q) bidentate directing group is essential. Induction period observed in most runs. Cathodic half-reaction is H2 evolution.
